name: Dynamic Programming
def: Solves problems with overlapping subproblems and optimal substructure by reusing subresults.
problem: Avoid exponential recomputation when a problem decomposes into reused, overlapping subproblems.
mechanism: Define a recurrence over states; cache results via top-down memoisation or bottom-up tabulation, often reducing dimensionality for space.
benefits: Turns exponential brute force into polynomial time
Space can often be reduced to O(1) rows
limits: State space can still be huge
failures: Memory blow-up from over-large state tables
myths: "DP = memoisation" — memoisation is one implementation; tabulation is the other
